debug_named(3) Library Functions Manual debug_named(3)

NAME

debug_named - Debug_named

SYNOPSIS

Files


file named.c
Named object registry for debugging. file named.h
Named object registry for debugging — log identifiable resource names.

Type-Specific Description Macros

These macros encapsulate the proper casting and type hint for common types. Use these instead of NAMED_DESCRIBE for better readability and consistency.
#define NAME_DESCRIBE_MUTEX(m)
Describe a mutex_t for logging. #define NAME_DESCRIBE_RWLOCK(l)
Describe an rwlock_t for logging. #define NAME_DESCRIBE_COND(c)
Describe a cond_t for logging. #define NAME_DESCRIBE_THREAD(t)
Describe a thread for logging. #define NAME_DESCRIBE_TRANSPORT(tr)
Describe a transport for logging. #define NAME_DESCRIBE_SOCKET(fd)
Describe a socket for logging. #define NAME_DESCRIBE_RINGBUF(rb)
Describe a ring buffer for logging.

Detailed Description

This module provides a centralized registry for naming any addressable resource: mutexes, threads, sockets, ring buffers, network connections, etc.

Any value that fits in a uintptr_t can be registered with a human-readable name. Names are auto-suffixed with a unique ID (e.g., "recv_mutex.7") to distinguish multiple instances of the same conceptual resource.

The registry is thread-safe and zero-overhead in release builds (NDEBUG).

Note

Registry is not thread-safe for unregistration during unwind. Always unregister resources before they become invalid (e.g., in destroy/cleanup).

USAGE EXAMPLES:

// Pointer-based (for heap objects, structs) ringbuffer_t *rb = ringbuffer_create(...); NAMED_REGISTER(rb, "recv_audio"); // auto name: "recv_audio.3" // ... use rb ... NAMED_UNREGISTER(rb); ringbuffer_destroy(rb);

// Integer handle-based (for fd, socket, etc.) int client_fd = accept(server_fd, ...); NAMED_REGISTER_ID(client_fd, "client_socket"); // ... use client_fd ... NAMED_UNREGISTER_ID(client_fd); close(client_fd);

// Describing in logs log_info("Data received on %s", NAME_DESCRIBE_SOCKET(fd)); // Output: "socket: client_socket.2 (0x8)"

Author

Zachary Fogg me@zfo.gg

Date

February 2026

#define
  NAMED_GET_BY_INT( id, buffer, size)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/debug/named.h>Value:


do {                                                                                                                 \


  const char *_name = named_get((uintptr_t)(id));                                                                   \


  if (_name) {                                                                                                       \


    strncpy((buffer), (_name), (size) - 1);                                                                         \


    (buffer)[(size) - 1] = '\0';                                                                                    \


  } else {                                                                                                           \


    snprintf((buffer), (size), "0x%lx", (unsigned long)(id));                                                       \


  }                                                                                                                  \
} while (0)






Get name of an integer ID or format as hex fallback.


Parameters


id The integer ID to look up (e.g., thread ID, FD,
  packet type)


buffer Output buffer for the result


size Size of the output buffer


Tries to look up the registered name for an integer ID. If found,
    copies it to buffer. If not found, formats the ID as "0x%lx"
    (hexadecimal).




Use this for thread IDs and other small integer identifiers. In
    release builds (NDEBUG), always formats as hex.

#define
  NAMED_GET_BY_PTR( key, buffer, size)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/debug/named.h>Value:


do {                                                                                                                 \


  const char *_name = named_get(key);                                                                               \


  if (_name) {                                                                                                       \


    strncpy((buffer), (_name), (size) - 1);                                                                         \


    (buffer)[(size) - 1] = '\0';                                                                                    \


  } else {                                                                                                           \


    snprintf((buffer), (size), "0x%tx", (ptrdiff_t)(key));                                                          \


  }                                                                                                                  \
} while (0)






Get name of a pointer/key or format as address fallback.


Parameters


key The uintptr_t key to look up


buffer Output buffer for the result


size Size of the output buffer


Tries to look up the registered name for the key. If found, copies
    it to buffer. If not found, formats the key as "0x%tx"
    (hexadecimal address).




Useful in hot paths like logging where you want the name if
    available, but need a reasonable fallback for unregistered keys.




In release builds (NDEBUG), always formats as address.

1039       (buffer)[(size) - 1] = '\0';                                                                                    \

1041       snprintf((buffer), (size), "0x%tx", (ptrdiff_t)(key));                                                          \

Copy a string to buffer, with bounds checking.


Parameters


str The string to copy (may be NULL)


buffer Output buffer


size Size of the output buffer


Safe string copy that handles NULL inputs and ensures null
    termination. Useful for formatting optional or variable string fields.




In release builds (NDEBUG), copies the string as-is.

#define
  NAMED_OPEN( pathname, name, flags, ...)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/debug/named.h>Value:


({                                                                                                                   \


  int _fd;                                                                                                           \


  if ((flags) & O_CREAT) {                                                                                           \


    _fd = platform_open((pathname), (flags), __VA_ARGS__);                                                           \


  } else {                                                                                                           \


    _fd = platform_open((pathname), (flags));                                                                        \


  }                                                                                                                  \


  if (_fd >= 0) {                                                                                                    \


    named_register_fd(_fd, NULL, __FILE__, __LINE__, __func__);                                                            \


  }                                                                                                                  \


  _fd;                                                                                                               \
})






Open a file and register the file descriptor.


Parameters


pathname File path to open


name Debug name for the file descriptor


flags Open flags (O_RDONLY, O_WRONLY, etc.)


... Optional mode argument for O_CREAT


Convenience macro that opens a file and registers the returned FD.
    Automatically uses "%d" format specifier for file descriptor
    integers.




Usage: int fd = NAMED_OPEN("/path/to/file",
    "myfile", O_RDONLY); int fd =
    NAMED_OPEN("/path/to/file", "myfile", O_CREAT |
    O_WRONLY, 0644);

696       _fd = platform_open((pathname), (flags), __VA_ARGS__);                                                           \
697     } else {                                                                                                           \
698       _fd = platform_open((pathname), (flags));                                                                        \

700     if (_fd >= 0) {                                                                                                    \
701       named_register_fd(_fd, NULL, __FILE__, __LINE__, __func__);                                                            \

703     _fd;                                                                                                               \
704   })










#define
  NAMED_REGISTER( ptr, name, type, fmt, parent_ptr)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/debug/named.h>Value:


named_register((uintptr_t)(const void *)(ptr), (name), (type), (fmt), __FILE__, __LINE__, __func__, (uintptr_t)(const void *)(parent_ptr))






Register any pointer with base name, type, format spec, and
    location (auto-suffix).


Parameters


ptr Object pointer (void* or typed pointer)


name Base name string


type Data type label (e.g., "mutex", "socket")


fmt Format specifier for printing (e.g., "0x%tx")


In debug builds, automatically captures FILE, LINE,
    and func for location info. In release builds (NDEBUG), this is a
    no-op.

const
  char * named_describe (uintptr_t key, const char * type_hint)


#include
  <include/ascii-chat/debug/named.h>


Format a description string for logging.


Parameters


key The resource key


type_hint A type label (e.g., "mutex", "socket",
  "thread")


Returns


Description string in format "type_hint: name
  (0xKEY)" or type_hint if not registered


Returns a per-thread static buffer suitable for a single log call.
    The buffer is overwritten on the next call to this function within the same
    thread. Do not store or free the returned pointer.




If the key is not registered, returns just the type_hint. In
    release builds (NDEBUG), this always returns "?".


Definition at line 353 of file debug/named.c.


353                                                                  {
354   if (!type_hint) type_hint = "object";
355 
356   static _Thread_local char buffer[DESCRIBE_BUFFER_SIZE];
357 
358   if (!lifecycle_is_initialized(&g_named_registry.lifecycle)) {
359     snprintf(buffer, sizeof(buffer), "%s (0x%tx)", type_hint, (ptrdiff_t)key);
360     return buffer;
361   }
362 
363   rwlock_rdlock(&g_named_registry.entries_lock);
364   named_entry_t *entry = NULL;
365   HASH_FIND(hh, g_named_registry.entries, &key, sizeof(uintptr_t), entry);
366   rwlock_rdunlock(&g_named_registry.entries_lock);
367 
368   if (entry) {
369     const char *type = entry->type ? entry->type : type_hint;
370     const char *name = entry->name ? entry->name : "unknown";
371     if (entry->file && entry->func && entry->line > 0) {
372       snprintf(buffer, sizeof(buffer), "%s/%s (0x%tx) @ %s:%d:%s()", type, name, (ptrdiff_t)key,
373                entry->file, entry->line, entry->func);
374     } else {
375       snprintf(buffer, sizeof(buffer), "%s/%s (0x%tx)", type, name, (ptrdiff_t)key);
376     }
377   } else {
378     snprintf(buffer, sizeof(buffer), "%s (0x%tx)", type_hint ? type_hint : "unknown", (ptrdiff_t)key);
379   }
380 
381   return buffer;
382 }

    named_entry::line, named_entry::name, rwlock_rdlock,
    rwlock_rdunlock, and named_entry::type.

void
  named_destroy (void )


#include
  <include/ascii-chat/debug/named.h>


Destroy the named object registry. Cleans up all registered names
    and internal structures. Typically called from lock_debug_destroy().


Definition at line 101 of file debug/named.c.


101                          {
102   // Always attempt to shut down the lifecycle, but always cleanup entries
103   lifecycle_shutdown(&g_named_registry.lifecycle);
104 
105   rwlock_wrlock(&g_named_registry.entries_lock);
106 
107   // Find the registry's own rwlock entry (allocated during named_init bootstrap)
108   // This entry will be unregistered by rwlock_destroy, so we must not free it here
109   uintptr_t registry_lock_key = (uintptr_t)(const void *)&g_named_registry.entries_lock;
110   named_entry_t *registry_lock_entry = NULL;
111   HASH_FIND(hh, g_named_registry.entries, &registry_lock_key, sizeof(uintptr_t), registry_lock_entry);
112 
113   for (named_entry_t *e = g_named_registry.entries; e != NULL;) {
114     named_entry_t *next = e->hh.next;
115 
116     // Skip the registry lock entry - it will be cleaned up by rwlock_destroy
117     // which calls NAMED_UNREGISTER after we release the lock
118     if (e == registry_lock_entry) {
119       e = next;
120       continue;
121     }
122 
123     // Free all other entries
124     free(e->name);
125     if (e->type) free(e->type);
126     if (e->format_spec) free(e->format_spec);
127     if (e->file) free(e->file);
128     if (e->func) free(e->func);
129     free(e);
130     e = next;
131   }
132 
133   rwlock_wrunlock(&g_named_registry.entries_lock);
134 
135   // rwlock_destroy will call NAMED_UNREGISTER on the lock entry, which will:
136   // 1. Acquire the lock (now safe since we released it)
137   // 2. Find and remove the lock entry from the hash
138   // 3. Free the lock entry's fields
139   rwlock_destroy(&g_named_registry.entries_lock);
140 }






References lifecycle_shutdown(), rwlock_destroy(),
    rwlock_wrlock, and rwlock_wrunlock.


Referenced by asciichat_shared_destroy().

asciichat_error_t named_init (void )


#include
  <include/ascii-chat/debug/named.h>


Initialize the named object registry.


Returns


0 on success, non-zero on error


Must be called once at startup before any named_register()
    calls. Typically called from lock_debug_init().


Definition at line 82 of file debug/named.c.


82                                    {
83   // Idempotent: if already initialized, return success
84   if (lifecycle_is_initialized(&g_named_registry.lifecycle)) {
85     return ASCIICHAT_OK;
86   }
87 
88   if (!lifecycle_init(&g_named_registry.lifecycle, "named_registry")) {
89     return ASCIICHAT_OK;
90   }
91 
92   if (rwlock_init(&g_named_registry.entries_lock, "named_registry_lock") != 0) {
93     lifecycle_shutdown(&g_named_registry.lifecycle);
94     return ASCIICHAT_OK;  // Continue even if rwlock init fails
95   }
96

const
  char * named_register (uintptr_t key, const char * base_name, const char *
  type, const char * format_spec, const char * file, int line, const char *
  func, uintptr_t parent_key)


#include
  <include/ascii-chat/debug/named.h>


Register a resource with an auto-suffixed name, type, and location
    info.


Parameters


key A uintptr_t representing the resource (pointer
  or integer handle)


base_name The base name (e.g., "recv"); suffix auto-generated


type Data type label (e.g., "mutex", "rwlock",
  "socket"); stored and printed


file Source file where registration occurred (typically FILE)


line Source line where registration occurred (typically LINE)


func Function where registration occurred (typically func)


Returns


Pointer to the full registered name (e.g.,
  "recv.7"), valid until unregister


The returned name string is stored in the registry and remains
    valid until named_unregister(key) is called. Do not modify or free the
    returned pointer.




The type parameter is stored in the registry and used by
    named_describe() to format output as "type: name (0xKEY) @
    file:line:func()" automatically, without needing a type_hint
  parameter.




Multiple registrations of the same key will overwrite the previous
    entry. In release builds (NDEBUG), this is a no-op and returns
  base_name.


Definition at line 176 of file debug/named.c.


177                                                                                                {
178 #ifdef NDEBUG
179   // In release builds, skip debug registry and return the base name
180   return base_name ? base_name : "?";
181 #endif
182 
183   if (!base_name || !type || !format_spec) {
184     return "?";
185   }
186 
187   if (!lifecycle_is_initialized(&g_named_registry.lifecycle)) {
188     return base_name;
189   }
190 
191   // If parent_key provided, look up parent name and create hierarchical name
192   char final_base_name[256];
193   if (parent_key != 0) {
194     rwlock_rdlock(&g_named_registry.entries_lock);
195     const char *parent_name = named_lookup_name_unlocked(parent_key);
196     rwlock_rdunlock(&g_named_registry.entries_lock);
197 
198     if (parent_name) {
199       snprintf(final_base_name, sizeof(final_base_name), "%s#%s", parent_name, base_name);
200     } else {
201       // Parent not found, fall back to base name
202       snprintf(final_base_name, sizeof(final_base_name), "%s", base_name);
203     }
204   } else {
205     snprintf(final_base_name, sizeof(final_base_name), "%s", base_name);
206   }
207 
208   // Generate unique name using atomic counters (only for non-hierarchical names)
209   char name_buffer[256];
210   if (parent_key == 0) {
211     uint64_t counter = 0;
212     if (strcmp(type, "mutex") == 0) {
213       counter = __sync_fetch_and_add(&mutex_counter, 1);
214     } else if (strcmp(type, "rwlock") == 0) {
215       counter = __sync_fetch_and_add(&rwlock_counter, 1);
216     } else if (strcmp(type, "cond") == 0) {
217       counter = __sync_fetch_and_add(&cond_counter, 1);
218     } else if (strcmp(type, "atomic") == 0) {
219       counter = __sync_fetch_and_add(&atomic_counter, 1);
220     }
221     snprintf(name_buffer, sizeof(name_buffer), "%s.%"PRIu64, final_base_name, counter);
222   } else {
223     // Hierarchical names don't get counters - parent already has one
224     snprintf(name_buffer, sizeof(name_buffer), "%s", final_base_name);
225   }
226 
227   // Allocate entry BEFORE acquiring lock (avoid long critical section)
228   named_entry_t *entry = malloc(sizeof(named_entry_t));
229   if (!entry) return base_name;
230 
231   entry->key = key;
232   entry->name = entry_strdup(name_buffer);
233   named_entry_init(entry, type, format_spec, file, line, func);
234 
235   // Only hold lock for the hash table operation
236   log_dev("[NAMED_REGISTER_LOCK_1] About to acquire entries_lock for key=%p type=%s", (void*)key, type);
237   rwlock_wrlock(&g_named_registry.entries_lock);
238   log_dev("[NAMED_REGISTER_LOCK_2] ✅ Acquired entries_lock, adding hash entry");
239   HASH_ADD(hh, g_named_registry.entries, key, sizeof(uintptr_t), entry);
240   log_dev("[NAMED_REGISTER_LOCK_3] Hash entry added, releasing entries_lock");
241   rwlock_wrunlock(&g_named_registry.entries_lock);
242   log_dev("[NAMED_REGISTER_LOCK_4] ✅ Released entries_lock");
243 
244   return entry->name;
245 }

    lifecycle_is_initialized(), log_dev, named_entry::name,
    rwlock_rdlock, rwlock_rdunlock, rwlock_wrlock, and
    rwlock_wrunlock.






const
  char * named_register_fd (int fd, const char * name, const char * file, int
  line, const char * func)


#include
  <include/ascii-chat/debug/named.h>


Register a file descriptor with namespace encoding.


Parameters


fd File descriptor value


file Source file where registration occurred


line Source line where registration occurred


func Function where registration occurred


Returns


Registered name string


Registers an FD with a key that includes type namespace to avoid
    collisions with packet types or other numeric values. If name is non-NULL,
    uses "name (fd=N)" format; otherwise auto-generates as
    "fd=N". In release builds (NDEBUG), this is a no-op and returns
    "fd=%d" string.

439   if (fd < 0) {
440     return "?";
441   }
442 
443   if (!lifecycle_is_initialized(&g_named_registry.lifecycle)) {
444     return "?";
445   }
446 
447   char name_buffer[256];
448   if (name) {
449     snprintf(name_buffer, sizeof(name_buffer), "%s", name);
450   } else {
451     snprintf(name_buffer, sizeof(name_buffer), "fd=%d", fd);
452   }
453 
454   // Allocate entry BEFORE acquiring lock
455   named_entry_t *entry = malloc(sizeof(named_entry_t));
456   if (!entry) return "?";
457 
458   uintptr_t key = encode_fd_key(fd);
459   entry->key = key;
460   entry->name = entry_strdup(name_buffer);
461   named_entry_init(entry, "fd", "%d", file, line, func);
462 
463   // Only hold lock for the hash table operation
464   rwlock_wrlock(&g_named_registry.entries_lock);
465   HASH_ADD(hh, g_named_registry.entries, key, sizeof(uintptr_t), entry);
466   rwlock_wrunlock(&g_named_registry.entries_lock);
467 
468   return entry->name;
469 }

  char * named_register_fmt (uintptr_t key, const char * type, const char *
  format_spec, const char * file, int line, const char * func, const char * fmt,
  ...)


#include
  <include/ascii-chat/debug/named.h>


Register a resource with a formatted name, type, and location info
    (no auto-suffix).


Parameters


key A uintptr_t representing the resource


type Data type label (e.g., "mutex", "socket");
  stored and printed


file Source file where registration occurred


line Source line where registration occurred


func Function where registration occurred


fmt Printf-style format string


... Format arguments


Returns


Pointer to the full registered name


Allows full control over the registered name. The format string
    and arguments are printed to create the name (e.g., "client.%u"
    with args "17" → "client.17"). No auto-suffix
    counter is applied.




The type parameter is stored in the registry and used by
    named_describe() for automatic formatting without needing
  type_hint.




In release builds (NDEBUG), this is a no-op and returns
    "?".

250     return "?";

254     return fmt;
255   }
256 
257   // Format the name BEFORE acquiring lock
258   va_list args;
259   va_start(args, fmt);
260   char *full_name = NULL;
261   int ret = platform_vasprintf(&full_name, fmt, args);
262   va_end(args);
263 
264   if (ret < 0) {
265     return "?";
266   }
267 
268   // Allocate entry BEFORE acquiring lock
269   named_entry_t *entry = malloc(sizeof(named_entry_t));
270   if (!entry) {
271     free(full_name);
272     return "?";
273   }
274 
275   // Initialize entry (name is pre-allocated as full_name)
276   entry->key = key;
277   entry->name = full_name;
278   named_entry_init(entry, type, format_spec, file, line, func);
279 
280   // Only hold lock for the hash table operation
281   rwlock_wrlock(&g_named_registry.entries_lock);
282   HASH_ADD(hh, g_named_registry.entries, key, sizeof(uintptr_t), entry);
283   rwlock_wrunlock(&g_named_registry.entries_lock);
284 
285   return entry->name;
286 }

void
  named_registry_for_each (named_iter_callback_t callback, void *
  user_data)


#include
  <include/ascii-chat/debug/named.h>


Iterate through all registered entries.


Parameters


callback Function to call for each entry


user_data Opaque context passed to callback


Safely iterates through all registered entries without holding the
    lock for the entire iteration (entries are copied). Callback is invoked for
    each entry. In release builds (NDEBUG), this is a no-op.


Definition at line 394 of file debug/named.c.


394                                                                               {
395   if (!callback || !lifecycle_is_initialized(&g_named_registry.lifecycle)) {
396     return;
397   }
398 
399   // Snapshot entries while holding read lock
400   named_iter_entry_t entries[256];
401   int count = 0;
402 
403   rwlock_rdlock(&g_named_registry.entries_lock);
404   for (named_entry_t *e = g_named_registry.entries; e != NULL && count < 256; e = e->hh.next) {
405     entries[count].key = e->key;
406     strncpy(entries[count].name, e->name ? e->name : "?", MAX_NAME_LEN - 1);
407     entries[count].name[MAX_NAME_LEN - 1] = '\0';
408     count++;
409   }
410   rwlock_rdunlock(&g_named_registry.entries_lock);
411 
412   // Call callback outside the lock
413   for (int i = 0; i < count; i++) {
414     callback(entries[i].key, entries[i].name, user_data);
415   }
416 }

    named_iter_entry_t::name, rwlock_rdlock, and
    rwlock_rdunlock.

#include
  <include/ascii-chat/debug/named.h>


Register all packet types from packet_type_t enum.
    Registers all packet type enum values in the named registry using keys in
    the format "PACKET_TYPE=%d" where d is the enum value. This should
    be called once during initialization to enable packet type identification in
    log message formatting.

#include

Unregister a resource by key.


Parameters


key The same uintptr_t passed to
  named_register


Removes the resource from the registry. Safe to call multiple

289   if (!lifecycle_is_initialized(&g_named_registry.lifecycle)) {
290     return;
291   }
292 
293   rwlock_wrlock(&g_named_registry.entries_lock);
294   named_entry_t *entry = NULL;
295   HASH_FIND(hh, g_named_registry.entries, &key, sizeof(uintptr_t), entry);
296   if (entry) {
297     HASH_DEL(g_named_registry.entries, entry);
298     free(entry->name);
299     if (entry->type) free(entry->type);
300     if (entry->format_spec) free(entry->format_spec);
301     if (entry->file) free(entry->file);
302     if (entry->func) free(entry->func);
303     free(entry);
304   }
305   rwlock_wrunlock(&g_named_registry.entries_lock);

const
  char * named_update_name (uintptr_t key, const char * new_base_name)


#include
  <include/ascii-chat/debug/named.h>


Update the registered name for a resource with a new base
  name.


Parameters


key The resource key


new_base_name New base name (will be auto-suffixed with counter)


Returns


Updated name string, or NULL if key not found


Updates an existing registration with a new base name. The new
    name will be auto-suffixed with a counter to make it unique (e.g.,
    "client_123.0"). Useful when a resource gets an ID assigned after
    creation (e.g., client_id).




In release builds (NDEBUG), this is a no-op and returns NULL.
